review_text:
Big park ....
All the ammenities
Pool, splash park,play areas....
Currently doing reno's minimal disruption to patrons...
Nice sites   wouldcome back

Arrived in the afternoon for check in. Asked for a unpowered site semi close to amenities. Thankfully granted. Now here's where the loose almost all stars. Price for an unpowered site on Saturday is $47. Standard rate is $30. We didn't argue at first due to being on the road all day driving. But in the morning we questioned it. And showed them the prices online. This $17 jump isn't right. I've sent a complaint through to discover parks. Not happy with that price jump. Do better discovery.

We came here pre-booked for a superior 2 bedroom cabin. With a carport. Nice veranda & comfortable outdoor chairs/table. A little rough around the edges, but very clean and comfortable/maintained cabin. The front room has 2 double bunks. And a small fan. The back room has large double bed with a big double mirrored cupboard, with sliding doors. And two side tables and lamps. Also A/C . The bathroom is of good size and shower is too. Good hot water and pressure, toilet is in the same room. The dining room, kitchen and lounge is of good size. With A/C & ceiling fan. Small family fridge, 2 burner gas stove. Most pots and pans, utensils etc. big solid timber table & six chairs and comfortable 3 seater lounge. Small tv but not tiny. Smoke alarms. All remotes work. We needed something fixed in the cabin and they came within the hour. We would have no problem recommending this cabin. And beautiful grass sites for holiday makers with caravans and tents. And quite a few sites with shaded trees. And if you’re in the Table Lands of Far North QLD, I recommend Atherton Holiday Park and Caravans. And the NRMA Bowen Beach Front Holiday Park. And the Discovery park in Dubbo, NSW. 5 minutes from Dubbo Zoo.

Great spot for people with children.Great Keppel Sands is nearby.Great fishing spots close by.Easy access to 4 wheel drive at Farnborough Beach and five rocks.Day trips to the Byfield national parks,all,this the hub for all these activities and more.

The accomodation was neat and tidy, pools were great fun for the entire family.  Lacking in bbq areas and the bloke at the cafe everytime went there was flustered, expressing how busy he was and unsure if could accommodate our orders!
Come across abit rude in his manner! But aside from that is a very nice holiday park

We are quite experienced travelers and have stayed in many places around the world whether in a camper, while backpacking, or in hotels.||The surroundings of the park are beautiful and definitely invite you to relax and spend some time there. In order to fully enjoy our vacation, we booked a cabin which, according to the website, was supposed to be perfect for couples looking for a romantic stay.||Unfortunately the cabin we received was in very poor condition and looked quite run-down. It smelled old, dusty, and musty. The bed was on wheels that could no longer be locked, so it rolled around the room with even the slightest movement. The mattresses seemed very old and worn out, causing you to sink in deeply.||The bunk bed looked as if the bedding had not been changed. The chairs were worn, uncomfortable, and not pleasant to look at. The bathroom had an extremely old shower, moldy roof, leaking windows, and several insects.||Most of the cabin’s windows could not be locked, were slightly open, protectet by defective insect screen and could easily be opened from the outside. Ironically, the park advertises the slogan “Lock it or lose it” on the flyer handed out at check-in.||By the evening, our cabin had unfortunately also become home to many insects even though the doors and windows were closed. After finally falling asleep following an extensive insect hunt, we were woken up by a large cockroach crawling across our bed. We then found several more throughout the cabin. They most likely came from the unpleasant looking drain or through the poorly sealed doors and windows with broken insect screens.||Unfortunately the photos on the website did not match the cabin we were given. The park cleverly mentions online that some cabins may look different and have different furnishings.||The price of AUD 240 for two nights is absolutely not justified for this type of accommodation. If we had known the true condition of the cabin or that it did not match what we had booked, we would never have made a stop here in Yeppoon.||We had originally booked two nights, but after this experience we decided not to stay for the second night and have already booked another accommodation.||This morning we informed reception about our dissatisfaction and checked out. We were assured that the manager would contact us later that day but unfortunately we have not heard anything from management so far.||Our tip: It’s better to spend your hard earned money on a different accommodation and save yourself an unpleasant experience.☺️
So disappointing, this place is nowhere close to Yeppoon. No food options within walking distance. Can't use the beach. Staff dismissed me when I asked questions.  Not happy at all.
We always love staying here, such a perfect family getaway. This time, we tried the cafe, the pizza and calamari was AMAZING! Do yourself a favour and try some.

We had no hot water on the Friday night, kids were cold out of the pool and my partner is disabled with back issues and the cold water caused her to have back spasms and nerve pain was terrible all weekend for her, not real good for her birthday. After hours maintenance came along and said we will have to wait for morning for a fix, he mentioned that he agrees it’s unexceptionable and said the manager is a “cool guy” and would fix us up for the inconvenience. The maintenance man arrived just after 9am Saturday and put a temporary HWS in place to see us out fire the stay which we appreciated on the belief that management were still going to contact us for some sort of compensation. We heard nothing so on departure I mentioned it to reception and a man came out from the office ( possibly manager not sure didn’t introduce himself) he said but they put a replacement in on Saturday morning didn’t they?. I said yes but what about Friday night and Saturday morning showers? No reply. I mentioned the price was double of the same booking last year so it was indexable

The campersite had lots of grass and was perfectly level. Bushes break up the campsite so you are not looking directly into the next camper. Check-in was as smooth-as from very pleasant staff. Although close to the beach, the beach access from the campsite is not that easy, and it is probably easier to drive to a beach if that is what you are are after. The campsite totally comes into its own with the pool/kids area - quite possibly one of the best we have seen. A large pool with slope access (and places for the kids to jump in). There is plenty of grass for parents to sit and watch their little ones. A smaller pool with a great water slide which also has covered lounger areas where parents can sit and watch. There was also a water park for the small kids. The people camping next to us had come with their children just for the water park and had no intention of leaving the site. There is also a small cafe (we didn't use) that does food on some nights if you don't want to cook. Utilities were clean. The camp kitchen looked a little light on the equipment for the size of the park, but as we never had to wait for anything they must have it right for the patrons that use the park.
Liked our stay at the park.

NOT CHILD FRIENDLY and do not take your kids if they ever make a mistake.
Also there was a creepy man literally watching my 12 year old sister everytime she went to the water park and nothing got done when complained about.
Doing a load of washing there also cost $18 per load ontop of the RIDICULOUS room price and the bedding had to be washed my Mums first day there before even being used after the cleaners should’ve been as it literally still smelled so she got to pay almost $20 to do a job people there are paid to do.
This place is awful! My Mum paid for a cabin for 2 weeks for with my three younger siblings aged 10-15 because they had no where else to stay between moving homes and the cabin they got had a cracked tile which she hurt herself on and hadn’t even complained due to literally not having any other options as well as the pricing is RIDICULOUS for how little they look after their guest. (2 week booked stay and 3 kids on the booking they left 2 rolls of toilet paper, 1 garbage bag and 1 hot chocolate packet so first day there the kids couldn’t even have a hot chocolate each which might not seem like a big thing but was still upsetting when they are so thrown off already) then a incident occurred where my younger brother was riding his E scooter around (judge him all you want but no one gets up the guest hooking in and out of there at 20km per hour in a 5km zone and his E scooter isn’t even able to go near that fast.) and he almost ran into another kid who ran out into the middle of the path and he ended up stacking it, he swore (probably because he got hurt) and then still even apologized to the kid who then went and told their parents who also then went and complained to the owner (ok that’s fine go have a talk to my brother and my Mum and if it’s so much of an issue tell him he can’t ride the scooter and while he was at it, could’ve said something to the people who could’ve killed a child this morning hooking out at 20-25km per hour in a 4WD and whatever the little car behind it was) but instead evicted them for 10am the next morning and is going to refund their money which will take at LEAST until next week to happen so stranded a single working mother and 3 children out onto the street without any other warnings given. Don’t holiday here unless you can afford to stay somewhere else if the owner decides to have a disgusting power trip and abuse their position because she booked for a longer time and got a extremely SMALL discount on the accommodation and if he evicts her he can rent the cabin out to other people for shorter times at a higher fee. The cabin she was paying $1500  per week for also had a broken tile which injured her and a child which she had to tape over so it didn’t hurt anyone else. DISGUSTING on the owners behalf even knowing her situation to do this without even warning her and I will be making sure to report the safety hazard. Extra disgusting to evict them with barely 12 hours notice knowing all her money is in the businesses pocket and she won’t even be able to afford somewhere that will treat them better now.
Nothing got done about someone ogling a 12 year old girl in swimmers but crucified my younger brother and left him in tears blaming himself because now his Mum and siblings are homeless and don’t even have money to be able to stay else where.

The manager Tony is excellent, staff in the office are friendly, happy and always welcoming. Amenities are always clean, lots to do for kids. Holiday activities are well thought through. Any problems are quickly attended too which is rare this time of the year.
We recommend to families this time of the year.

I booked a van site for 3 nights, within an hour and a half of booking I suffered a traumatic leg injury that excluded me from walking let alone going caravanning. I immediately rang to explain my predicament and that I would have to cancel my booking.

I admit it was within thier 14 day cut off for a refund, however considering it was within 2 hours of booking and then cancelling, it would be highly unlikely I have done them out of a booking on that site for someone else.
I just find it highly un empathetic given my situation and plain greedy not to refund me.
I’m a local to the area and would have stayed there at some other time but I highly doubt I’ll be doing that or recommend them to any of my travelling friends. Heaps of other parks in the area.

Update…..
After posting this review. Cool Waters Park responded and said they would call me and discuss the situation.
They didn’t contact me at all, instead, they removed their input to my review saying they would call me.

网上所有的图片都是实拍图，没有任何美化，所见即所得，赞👍。

房间干净整洁，泳池也非常干净舒适，户外探索乐趣多。

所有的工作人员都热情，友善又专业，给我们带来非常好的体验。我到达的时间太晚，错误得打电话给应急号码，应急人员非常友善地指引我入住，后来我才发现她们将我所有需要用到的信息帮我放在门口保险箱，非常抱歉同时也非常感动。离开的上午我的车辆打不着火，前台帮我们叫来了其他工作人员帮我的车接电，这不是她们的责任，但是她们非常热心的帮助了我，她们的热情和友善是无法比拟的。
Awesome park
Absolutely loved the park had a great stay with my family definitely recommend
I camped over the kings long weekend in a tent with my 3 children. Great vibe safe and secure. Staff warm, friendly and very accommodating.
Wonderful Destination for Camping Cabins, Recreation. School holiday activities happening.. Fiord watching the sailboats, or enjoy have a fish.. All Here🌻
Can anyone tell me how much per night for 2 on unpowered and powered site please ? Thanks, Jeff.
Friendly, quiet park. Plenty of space for everyone, lots of holiday makers but very quiet even with pool and slide just across from us. Amenities clean and good

Lovely resort in a good location and it is very well run. Booking in was easy and everything was well explained to us. Our budget bungalow was ready and clean when we arrived. The bungalow had everything we needed and was well equipped for budget accommodation.

The resort was remarkably quiet for the number of people and quite spacious.
Lovely park,friendly staff, great facilities and close to points of interest

Beautiful place to stay for camp. We stayed 2 nights there in an unpowered site. We were with our tent and we had plenty of room ! We were very happy with our place and really enjoyed the swimming pool also. Toilets and showers were also always clean. Just a little negative point was the kitchen that was outside without cookers and during windy or rainy days there is no inside place to cook or dine. There is dining room but was closed and just opened one night for a bus of children with their dinner activities. But this apart, everything was nice.
Fabulous caravan/park lot's of room all amenities very clean & tidy.  The best swimming pool water slide & water park for the little & big kid's, highly recommend 👌 👍 😀 👏
Coolwaters yeppoon the only park to go to we have been there a few times now,  awesome beautiful park , the staff are AMAZING SUCH A FANTASTIC TEAM , A HUGE THANKYOU TO YOU ALL WE WILL BE BACK 🐟🐟

Great place for family's and friends to hang out, fun activities to do for kids (or adults) while there include the water park with slides, jumping pillow and playground. Also walking distance from the causeway lake and beach/ocean.

Unpowered sites the biggest rip off!!! This area of unlevel dirt/mud, costed us $65 per night. Rain was well and truly forecast but they still gave us a site located in a hole. No camp kitchen or amenities close by for those that would get most use out of them. Had to set up our own portable toilet in a tent as the toilets were to far for the kids to make it. Very poorly laid out park. Very disappointed. Unmaintained dirt tracks to the camp site. BMX track was pathetic, very small patch with a few tiny lumps of dirt, hardly a bmx track at all.
Nice spot, friendly people
Seems like a well loved place to the regulars. Everything spick n span, with a well used waterpark.
Most amazing place to camp with the family already booked our next stay

We arrived to find our booking was mistakenly for 1 month later. However with a bit of discussion we were offered an upgrade on an en suite site. We couldn't use the en suite the first night but that was no problem. Happy we didn't have to move mid stay. Excellent resolution

We left today after a 4 night stay on a supposed to be grass site. The day we arrived was raining and was like a mud bath, no fault to the park but if there was grass would have been much better. We tried to get TV reception and WiFi as advertised but there was none. If that was not bad enough we woke to see our caravan absolutely covered in Shit and Berry Dust from a tree that was above our van. We reported it to a grounds man the second  day which he was very nice and said he'd take it to the manager, also to another one this morning and was told that we didn't have to come. We paid our money the same as the ones on a beautiful site not to even get any response. If anyone books this park make sure you don't get site 11 or you'll spend all your time washing your van. I'm sure if we had a better site it would have been a much nicer stay

Great facilities, very nice pools, tennis court, jumping pillow, and ensuite.  Site unfortunately was a dust bowl with no slab and no grass.  The staff said they would do something about it but didn’t, but other than that was great stay.  Some people do go pretty fast in cars down the centre road so watch your kids.

Tidy clean well laid out park. Too expensive at $77.60/ night 2 adults power & water. We have a 22ft van & they charge for your site size. Haven't experienced this pricing style anywhere else.
Being on the coast they can charge what they like I guess & nothing else available. QLD school holidays had finished. We had booked 5 nights & booked an extra night & that night(a Sunday) was $81.60!!! WAY TO MUCH I SAY ... Also it’s a bit of a walk to the beach. Looks like it’s across the road but not accessible straight there.
Great park

We have a wonderful experience staying at the Coolwater, Yeppoon Discovery Parks. Our is a power site has a ensuite with it.  Inside has everything we need, like shampoo etc and even come with a washing machine and a drying rack, that is just perfect! The surrounding is very beautiful. We like our site is drive through as well. But more importantly we find the staff were very friendly and helpful. We have a problem tuning the TV and I tried to many times but no luck.  My husband then contact the on site groundman and Mat (the maintenance /groundman) came to help us out, within a short time, he just fixed for us. We find Mat so professional, so friendly, just an all around good guy.
We cannot said thank you enough.

We stayed in the non powered section in our camper, as an area designed for off-grid and camping I'm unsure why it's so far from the amenities-trapsing back and forth to the bathrooms and laundry was really tedious especially with little kids. The camp "kitchen" was also pretty poorly equipped. The pool and waterpark were good, as was the location.
Fantastic park, great amenities, very clean, welcoming and friendly staff, fantastic onsite cafe

well maintained facilities, plenty of activities to do from movie nights, bingo nights, arts and crafts for the kids, tennis court, basketball court, swimming pool & boat ramp right at the back. Bring a boat fishing rods at the same time enjoy a walk along the beach which is across the road. We had a great weekend away and are going back again 🤗.

Great place.  Clean, tidy. Safe, kid friendly, staff are amazing. Helped even with the little things like batteries for my grandsons bubble blower to finding some out the back and putting them in (thankyou janelle) to helping this old nan on one of the pedal cars  .  Thankyou.  Only one complaint was that the internet was bad out here, so I couldn't get any work done.  Highly recommended.  Never been here before but will come again when I visit grandson

Very nice park. Amenities very clean. Activities available for kids for gold coin. Room for larger vans. My only gripe is that the pool water is not heated. Could have far more use of the pools, like aquarobics and snorkelling lessons for kids, as other parks have.

Recently stayed here on a standard cabin for 3 nights. We really impressed with the cabin basic but clean and quite a bit of room.  Was winter so did not swim in the pools but hope to book and  come back in summer so I can enjoy more of the facilities . The young lady who checked us in was  very pleasant and help full

The deluxe cabins are spacious and in a more secluded area of the cp.
There is direct access to the river/lake and beach not far away.

What a fabulous park, the staff were awesome relocating us after the first night to a site that had sunshine galore. The first one was surrounded by trees and was a little chilly. The surrounding beach and lake are so close.  The park has a great Cafe which makes a good pizza when you don't feel like cooking. The park has a fantastic water park for kids. There's a fish n chip shop just across the Causeway from the park, that did great fish n chips 😋! This was a great relaxing place to stay. Stayed in our motorhome so didn't use the cabins. Most of the powered sites have there own ensuite, which was very clean, and much appreciated. Will call back in on another trip.

We took a bike ride into Yeppoon, to buy supplies, most of the ride was walking/bike shared zone. What gorgeous scenery along the way. We rode to Emu Park for supplies, although the ride was a little hazardous with most of the ride sharing it with cars.

Absolutely loved our 4 nights staying here. Amazing location for the kids. Well maintained and clean amenities. Although the place is a little older than other holiday parks I've visited the rooms were still clean and well maintained, and the park overall didn't feel tired or run down. We stayed in a cabin and it was well fitted out to be self sufficient, with a gas stove top, cutlery and cups and plates, microwave etc. There are clean bbqs as well in a few locations throughout the park. The pool area is quite well thought out, with the playground and pools all near each other. No needing to have to walk accross the park when the kids want to bounce from one thing to another. Nice and close to the beach (walk accross the road), and short driving distance to Emu Park. There's a boat ramp at the park also to access Causeway Lake.
Staff are very helpful and friendly.
They have an on-site Cafe as well. We had the pizza and it was delicious with Home made bases and sauce, and heaps of topping. They also have some locally made products for sale which I think is a lovely touch, very community minded.
100% would stay here again.

Grate Park. Wee stayed in a cabin. It was clean, roomy and a grate base for visiting local attractions. Fishing was gud in the creek. No license needed in Qld eh. Definately head back this weigh won day.
Excellent place to stay
This is a really nice park! Fairly quiet during our stay,  but really appreciated it.

This is a really nice park. The staff are very good and the sites are well maintained. Power and water are available and the toilets are cleaned and well appointed. There is a restaurant on site if you want take away or Pizza. This sits alongside a lake you can fish on has a water park, pool, BBQ and eating area. Very secure with reasonable access to a nice beach.
Nice family park. Great spot for kids and fishing.

The staff were welcoming and the park is kept immaculate. It’s a few km’s out of Yeppoon but that’s what I liked about it.
Amenities were fine and plenty of space at the caravan sites.

We loved it here. We were a group of 13, and had 2 eight person cabins. There was plenty of room for all of us. Lots for the children, and grandchildren to do. Right near the beach, and great for fishing.
The staff were lovely and so helpful.
There were a few issues. 1 of the public BBQ's was not working at all. 1 of the chairs in one of the units, fell apart when I was sitting on it. The air conditioner in the master bedroom was not working. I let them know all of this, and I am positive that they will take care of it. We will certainly be back there if we are in the area again.

Love this park and Yeppoon is the most beautiful town.
The staff at the park are really lovely and very helpful.
Definitely 5 stars, everything for kids.

My only problem was the  power and water pole are way too far away from the actual site. Lesson learnt carry an extra hose.

The caretaker helped us out and lent us one.

Top notch, love this area. Well worth the visit.
Lovely clean amenities. Bit noisy due to school holidays.
Staff very friendly and helpful, caravan sites are level and easy to access, amenities very clean.

We had such a great experience here. Stayed in a simple but perfect cabin. Slept the 4 of us and had absolutely everything we needed. Our boys loved the jumping pillow, water slide and modern pool. We also utilised the boat ramp and the view of the water at sunrise and sunset was perfection. Will definitely be returning. Lovely staff and highly recommend a game of tennis while there- lots of fun. Also not far from the  Turtle walk at Bluff Point, which is a must treck when in the area. The view is incredible!
Good little caravan park stayed on the ensuite site which is one of the best ensuites we have had, downside is a good 10-15 mins into Yeppoon

This place is absolutely amazing if you’re wanting a park that has it all. A huge collection of school holidays activities, great amenities and entertainment for the whole family. Will definitely be back
Great Park, cosy cabins. Was here for a school trip. Food was fantastic, service, excellent great location and facilities. Highly recommended.

My family and I absolutely love staying in our caravan at Coolwaters! The ensuite bathrooms are very modern and clean. The sites are massive for our 24ft van. The water pressure is great. Many activities for the kids, especially on school holidays. And everyone drives slow for the kids. We have stayed many times now.
Great stop over in our travels. Very cool pool with slide and amazing customer service
Good park, helpful staff and great pool area

What a wonderful relaxing stay. Amazingly clean park. Great service. Management went above and byond. Friendly staff even the meals were as better than any resturant. No need to leave once you arrive. will definitely be back higly recomended
Friendly staff, The caravan park is huge and has nice big sites. Great pools and waterslides.
Very enjoyable time had here, easy access to other areas from here including the ferry to Great Keppel Island. Good accommodation in the studio unit we had, and a good pool setup.

Beautiful spot. The park is well set out with something for everyone. Right on the beach and the causeway. We had fun unpowered site and decided to stay an extra day to our original booking. Great little cafe on site with friendly staff all over the park. Great pool and slides. Function rooms. Great spot. We will be back 😄

Disappointed that I wasn’t advised at tine of booking that we would be on sand rather than a concrete slab or grassed area
The other really disappointing thing is being parked along the back where the creek is it’s midge city you have to bath in spray to avoid being eaten alive
Ensuite needs a good clean dirty hand prints on walls and all sling the back & edge of the door
Shower refs a deep clean as well o understand they have a lot to do & more than likely on a time restriction for each one but maybe the management need to do spot checks every now and then
Don’t think we will be back to this park if we’re around this part of Qld again

We loved our stay at coolwaters the staff were amazing amenities were clean, |Kids loved all play equipment and pools were perfect,|The cafe Allamandas cafe across from pool was amazing |Best pizza I’ve ever had,|Morning coffee was amazing also
What a great park we got a fantastic deal on G'day . Great facilities and location . Staff have been fantastic . Onsite cafe is awsome .
Great place .  Will come back
Wonderful family friendly holiday park

We are on our 4th Christmas break at Coolwaters Yeppoon and once again our stay here has been nothing but awesome.
The park is always presented well, and the staff and grounds people have been nothing but accommodating and pleasant to deal with. The park manager is extremely kind helpful bloke, with nothing being a issue. Tony even offered my 2 children, and their friends all free rides on the Go Karts on New years day. Absolute champion and it was very appreciated.
We will be re booking here again as we love this place for our annual holiday.
Great job Coolwaters Yeppoon.

I recently had an incredibly disappointing experience at Coolwaters Yeppoon. The park managers were unprofessional and unnecessarily aggressive. They approached us on the road outside our campsite, loudly proclaiming their authority as managers and displaying alarming aggression, with the male manager flaring his nostrils and raising his voice.

Their dogs were barking and aggravating our Rottweiler, who was calmly lying on her belly, wanting to play and get pats. They falsely accused her of being aggressive, even though she showed zero signs of aggression. Just the day before, multiple kids from three different families ran up to us while I was walking her, asking to give her pats. She happily accepted the attention, responding with licks and affection.

The managers also falsely claimed there had been previous reports of our dog being aggressive. If this were true, why didn’t they address it earlier? Instead, they escalated the situation in front of multiple onlookers along the entire length of campsites, many of whom witnessed the incident. To make matters worse, the male manager aggressively attempted to kick our dog, which was entirely uncalled for.

As if that weren’t bad enough, they tried to charge us an additional $3,000, claiming they could change pricing whenever they wanted, despite the fact that we had paid for our sites well in advance. When we called to confirm the booking and payment, they insisted there was still an outstanding balance, which felt like a blatant money grab.

The unprofessionalism, dishonesty, and outright aggression from the park managers made this an appalling experience. I would strongly recommend avoiding this park if you’re looking for a fair and peaceful stay.
Multiple times we’ve stayed at this caravan resort and we’ve always had issues with lost payment and management. Resort owners/managers are dishonest and extremely, unprofessional.
The park is always immaculately clean. Having a slide is definitely a huge win for families!
Appalling customer service and extremely over priced for what you receive.

We stayed for 7 nights on an unpowered sites. The kids loved the jumping pillow and water park. The service was great by the staff, however the amenities could have been cleaned more regularly. On one occasion, there was a poo in a bucket next to the toilet. Not sure why on earth someone would do something like that. Staff were notified and cleaned it immediately. Overall, a great place for kids and big sites.

Good pool, splash park and slide. Please be aware that this is at Kinka beach, about 15 minutes drive outside Yepoon town centre. It is adjacent to 'The Causeway' which absolutely wreeks. As such the park has a rotting kelp smell most of the time that is pretty foul. Cleaner rocked up to clean ensuite just prior to 10am checkout, suggest departing well prior to 10am. If not the cleaner may try to enter whilst whilst facilities in use. Our van and car although hitched ready to leave were still onsite. Staff were all friendly, well maintained Caravan Park.

Well after arriving just on 7 pm on a Friday night we were delighted to see the main kitchen was offering pizza and pasta ,what a selection of beautiful food ,the pesto chicken pene was restaurant quality and for $15 ..Bonus !!! Definitely stopping in on our return visit
Initially had a lovely view of the lake from our site but the weekend brought in a lot more people. Facilities were very clean and tidy. Ideal place for families with plenty to entertain.

Missing the most important aspect of accomodation- good sleep!

So frustrating to pay $220 for a night of accommodation to have lights on all night outside which make it impossible to fall asleep and then sunlight shining in at 5am making it impossible to stay asleep. As a single mum traveling with 2 kids sleep is the most important thing as obviously have to drive and I am so beyond frustrated. It wouldn’t take much to invest in blackout curtains to avoid this problem.

Without this inconvenience I would give it 4 stars as the facilities are nice and the kids loved the pool and water play area, but unfortunately after a bad nights sleep nothing else matters.

The place needs a good deep clean as well, and the blind fell down completely  (luckily avoiding our heads) when I pulled it down.

Being so tired is unsafe and to call this deluxe is extremely misleading.

Unfortunately was disappointed with our stay. Booked months in advance and we were placed metres from the dump point 💩 which was not nice at all. There were people dumping 💩 throughout the night metres from us and could hear and smell it all. We had no shade and was blasted by the sun the whole stay. We were unable to be in our van for the middle of the day as it was ahot box. There were other lovely shaded empty lots. There were other sites available that were much nicer and Unfortunately we were given this one. We were visiting from Brisbane so a long drive so it was disappointing.  The ensuite we had was old and towel rail was broken and the sink was blocked and wouldn't drain yet all the other ensuites were new. We used the public amenities a couple of times as they were much nicer and the drain was not blocked. We Unfortunately had issues with noise and trucks driving past at 3am. We did like the pool and slides for the kids. However, there could be more shade on the outside of the pool area for people with dogs as they had to sit in the sun. A little sad to leave this review as we were really looking forward to a lovely stay.
Great location and amenities but dogs need policing.

Third year visiting coolwaters.
Always great service at the desk, clean, tidy and heaps to do! We love it here 🙌
Fantastic park for kids and a close walk to the beach.. Don’t forget mozzie spray especially if you’re planning to go to the movie nights, the mozzies were really bad near the palm trees.
Great park! Lots of activities to keep the kids entertained! My girls loved the daily 'Find the Koala 🐨' Pool and slide were a hit! Just wish the pool was a little warmer 🥶
Location and water park/pool is great. Pizza was yummy.
Best pizza in Yeppoon
There for 5 days and nights but only got clean towels on the 5th day.

Great Caravan Park. Unsuited sites are modern and clean.
Park amenities great for couples and families.
Amazing experience, in beautiful surrounds. I will be back for a longer stay next time!

Park appeared all o.k. We were at back with ensuites which was o.k.  The bath room excellent,..parking block spacious but missing a lot of TLC. Grass nearly gone.  Handy to both townships  & staff friendly.
We where just over nighters and you could have put us on the grass near the entrance. Instead of stack us in like sardines. Lucky to get our van in with all the cars there.
Not too busy in the unpowered sites and camped 30m from the lake.

We've loved our stay here and highly recommend this park. We had a large, well-grassed ensuite site and couldn't have been happier. The camp kitchen is one of the very best we've seen and the different food trucks each night was an unexpected and delicious bonus. The ensuite itself was the best of our travels to date. A great spot to stay!
Excellent caravan park. Great amenities

Staff are helpful and friendly, the park itself is very nice. But we were on site 4 which would be the worst site we have had anywhere, including free camps, in 22 years of caravanning. There are big tree roots and holes in the ground. It is very, very dangerous. Unfortunate enough to have rain during our stay and the mud pooled over the tree roots and made them even more slippery

The staff were so helpful and accommodating. Sites were tight, however the 8m grass sites were great! Very clean and quiet park, considering it was very busy. We could walk to the off-leash (4pm - 8am) dog beach across the road. Short drive to Yeppon or Emu Park. Amenities were clean and the pools looked fantastic! We had to change sites during our stay, the staff allowed us to park our van in a car park so we could still go to Great Keppel Island for the day.

Our stay was OK but I wouldn't go back or recommend it. Unfortunately the toilets/showers were discusting and unclean didn't see them get cleaned once while we stayed. There was also a awful sewage smell. Wasn't very fun for kids as in the afternoon things would get shut early and they weren't allowed to be used like the tennis court.
It would be OK if you were passing through and you needed somewhere for a night to stay.
Nice place to stay friendly staff

The slums of Mumbai smell better than this park.  It was sickening.   The amenities are like that of a 1970s caravan park and the budget toilet paper resulting in shredding as you try your pull it out of the roll holder.  This is not to the standard of a Discovery or G’day Park.
Administration girls, very friendly and helpful.Great amenities for all ages, definitely recommend to others and come back again.

What a great spot! Big and flat sites, clean and new amenities and a big kitchen with 2 stoves, an oven and a BBQ. Nice water playground and bouncing pillow. With only 32$ by far the cheapest site in Yeppoon.
Used a powered site. Park was clean and tidy. Amenities were clean, and staff were very helpful. Will stay again.
you a kept up to date with all the activities via Text.

This Caravan Park would satisfy most people’s needs as the park has various levels of cabin accommodation and spacious grassy caravan sites. The showers & toilets were clean and comfortable although we had an ensuite caravan site this stay. Kids would be fully catered for with the waterfall kids park and large pool. We would be happy to return to this caravan park again if in the region.

We booked 7 nights at this park for 2 adults and 1 pet, we chose this one because it was animal friendly.  On the afternoon of the 5th day a staff handyman noticed our cat sitting outside our door.  He immediately came over and said you can't have a cat here, we said we were under the assumption it was ok as it was pet friendly. Apparently a cat is not a pet, only dogs are. We were then addressed by the manager who said we needed to leave right then (4pm even though we had nowhere to go and our cat had not caused any problems).  Our cat is 11.5 years old, trained on a lead and is not a hunter.  None of this was acceptable to management.  She has been caravanning with us for 7 years and this has never been a problem, she sleeps inside all day and is walked each night on her lead.  Discovery, you need to do better, a family pet is not just a dog and some pf them ate actually therapy pets, and you need to be clearer and kinder.  We were left feeling so distressed and like criminals.  Be careful of this park if you have a cat, rat, bird or anything that is not a dog.

Claims to be pet friendly yet only Dogs allowed.  Little mention  of  other pets and does not specifically say cats not allowed.  It's main mention of pets is dogs not allowed in cabins. Anyway we took our cat which we keep in the van and only leave the site with her on a lead. To cut a long story short we were evicted when maintenance staff saw the  cat. No compromise, very authoritarian attitude. Choosing blind obedience of rules over understanding people is lowest on the scale of moral development.  It's the difference between running a prison or a caravan park.  We felt like we had committed a major crime.
Were those with dogs off leash evicted?- no
Were those breaking the noise curfew?- no
Were the permanents driving across the lawn instead of using  the road and shining headlights into our living space.
No ! And rightly so non of these should be evicted and neither should we.
Common sense over petty rules please!!!
Discovery parks were completely at fault  for claiming to be pet friendly and not clearly indicating no cats allowed. They then placed  all the onus/blame on us. We had to cut our stay short and find alternative accommodation at short notice.

We very much enjoyed our time and stay.

We chose to book a ensuite site, and was placed down the back in a court of ensuite sites.

Fantastic pool, waterslide, splash park, jumping pillow and small playground

We enjoyed the facilities and well kept  grounds

Only a short drive back into town and excellent beech across the road.

Reception staff super helpful and also great access to the river on one side of the park

The only negative, was our ensuite needed a really good clean! Yes it was nice and new, but not at all well cleaned at all, in despite need of some love!
Nice park excellent kids swimming pool and slides. Boat ramp on site and a short walk to a dog friendly beach.

Wir haben uns hier sehr wohl gefühlt. Wir waren außerhalb der Saison mit unseren Wohnmobil hier und alles war perfekt ruhig und annehmlich. Wir würden jederzeit wiederkommen und empfehlen es gern weiter.

Loved it be back again
So did my fur baby she loved it 2
Nice place to fish an see the reef

We booked a self contained apartment which was fully fly screened, fans and fully air conditioned. Three separate bedrooms which would sleep 8 people no problem. The kids absolutely loved it. Pools, slides, bouncing pillow, and organized activities.

A great location between Yeppoon and Emu Park, not too far from Rockhampton. From here we were able to easily take in all the great tourist type items and attractions including a visit to Great Kepple island to snorkel on the south end of the Great Barrier Reef.

Beautiful spot that's very kid-friendly. I HIGHLY recommend visiting Lianna out front at her coffee van, Hygge, for the best coffee, frappes, and treats around!  Within the park, there is a little water park, a giant bouncing pillow, and a balancing course that is open from morning to evening, all gated and suitable for children. For the adults, you can rent tennis gear from the office and use the tennis court. We also saw some little motorised buggies (single and double) you could rent.
Ja was soll ich sagen? Ein sehr sehr schöner Park. Hier hat es mir sehr gut gefallen und der Service sowie der Platz waren einfach super. Ich komme hier sehr gerne wieder hin.

We have stayed twice now with the kids it's a definite winner they 100% love it and the bed we had was super comfortable and the shower pressure is superb 👌. Can not wait to stay again in April for my daughters bday.

Will definitely be coming back with more family and friends, a great variety of things to do with kids such as 3 pools( 2 with slides), tennis court, basketball court, jumping pillow and hire out pedal carts, cabins are nice bit expensive for what they are i wish they would take that into consideration, camping spots / caravan sites are or the smaller side of sites.
Stayed in our van its a nice park but a little pricey and Min of 2 day stay which didn't really suit us
Great cabins and the park has something for everyone
Great place. Nice big sites and pet friendly 👍 🐶 and friendly staff 😊👌
When we went there in 2021 it was very busy but great, now we’re going again on the 23/12/2023 - 10/01/2024. Highly recommend.

Nice park. Pretty quiet and relaxing, plenty of space. Room we had had abit of a water view.

Only negatives being the cabin was abit out dated and oldish, could do with some updating, and a few other minor things, the kitchen had an ant problem, and there is no WiFi in the cabins too far from their "hotspots"

Overall nice place to visit.

Chalet-style site, our chalet was "Superior" and had clean tidy rooms, good shower and aircon with separate bedroom, shower, and kitchen/diner. Cooker, kettle, fridge freezer, microwave, dishwasher, but no laundry in room. Really nice sandy site, with noisy but beautiful cockatoos outside our chalet at 5am!

Best site we’ve stayed at!
We had an en-suite camper van spot that even had a washing machine!
Stunning views and super clean. (The beach across the road was fantastic)

There was even a food truck there on the night we stayed and the food was excellent!

Definitely recommend.

Cabins of varying sizes to accommodate a range of family sizes, starting from a single bedroom. Cabins all have a covered car port attached. Pool and jumping pillow on-site with peddle trikes for hire, too.
Nice quiet park walking distance to the beach and short drive to Yeppoon.

Great park, arrived late but enjoyed our time.
Kids loved the pools and slides.
great camping spot great service and pop up coffee shop out the front
A great place to stay, will be back for sure,

Just love this park and managers but the fibreglass stairs up to the water slide to the pool is SO noisy. It is a constant thump thump thump as kids go heavily up the fibreglass stairs
ALL...
DAY ....
LONG.. It is like bongo drums through the park it's that loud (and i'm not even near the water park).
Could Discovery Parks please install some deadening material to the steps to allow other park users some respite from the drumming ? School holidays I could not imagine ...😳
Cool waters is fantastic lots of resting and playing in the water slides and swimming pools, something for all ages
Discussed with staff. But everything else was great. Park has plenty to do. Amenities where clean
Very tidy and well set-up and friendly staff

Big park, good amenities for families with kids including big swimming pool, pool with slide, splash park, park, jumping pillow, tennis etc. Plus next door to a big lake.
Easy 8klm drive into Yeppoon town with new cinema, swimming lagoon and skate park etc.

Seriously this park couldn’t care less about the paying customers or their health. Smoking is encouraged with the feral community allowed to puff away within a few feet of others vans. |And the toilets , they don’t flush and you need to navigate your way through the previous unwashed croppers business. The sewerage stench by the pool encourages you to go home. Shame as the park is generally good and the ground man are top blokes.

Overall a very good park , however we had a powered site right at the end , lots of dirt and sand. Also the Landry at this end only has one working washing machine for lots of sites  , the others are all broken and in disrepair and have been for quite a while apparently. There are two clothes lines s one is in good condition the other needs re stringing so this is a bit annoying.

Sorry its long  but thats how bad it is,

Worst park we have ever stayed at, unless you are a regular who then dont have the rules apply to them, we were abused from the neightbour site ( apparently we were on his site after making comments about hitting our van with his boat on his way out and told his partner who cares they are on our site, to note they are not marked out) and then the on call staff came and was abused as well so finally park we went up to park  managment who refunded us our money and we left rather then applying the park rules of behavior, they are regulars to the park and we think this was the only reason they did not kick them out ( they also gad friends including the staff) I have never felt so unsafe in a park ever, I would stay in a dark corner in my van before going back. ( I told park managment i almost called the police it was that bad, responce was you could of, i found this a worry)
Also park is in the middle of no where and the staff are very cold and almost rude and the ensuite was absolutely disgusting and the sites are nothing like in the pictures, there is no grass its all black sand.
Other options are are at emu park and in yapoon itself both parks looked well maintained and more central.
Happy travels

Well overpriced. The amenities are outdated and dirty when we arrived, toilets dirty, mouse poo on the floor.
Our site didn't have a point where we could dump our water, to do washing up etc.. The only good thing is the waterpark for kids. 3 washing machines out of order and dryer out of order. Sites are quiet jam packed on top of each other also. Good location tho.

Ensuite powered site was on deep sand. No slab or lawn. A Bit messy...ensuite was nice. Shower noisy. Byo loo paper. Theirs is average. Good location for marina tour. Nice water park...and nice pool. |Firepit near our site attracted loud tipsy 75 year olds who chatted until 10pm.

Nice park to stay lots for the children to do
well appointed cabin daily updates via SMS
ran out of gas for the hot water while I was visiting on speaking to the office it was less than 10 minutes before someone arrived to change it over
friendly grounds men
Great place, lovley staff close to the beach, coffee van out the front for morning coffee 😍 and always something to do. 😍

We enjoyed our stay at Coolwaters. The amenities were great with plenty of hot water. The staff are friendly and reception is stocked with everything from bait to ice and gas. We loved our daily texts with details on community activities, food and coffee vans at the park.
Unfortunately no personal fires are allowed but the 3 communal fire pits are well serviced and stocked.
Book well in advance to avoid missing out.
While we don't have kids, this park would be the bees knees for them with a water park, tennis and jumping pillow on offer! If you have the opportunity to go to Bob's Bush Poetry, you'll be glad that you did!
Nice spot

Unfortunately, having booked a two week stay, we were unable to stay the second week due to serious health issues and the need to return home asap. |The staff advised refunds were not possible, a credit only could be issued and then only for Discovery Parks, not G’Day Parks.   |Given we are in our late 70’s, the health issue we are now dealing with, not having anyone else to pass this credit to and the likelihood travel will not be a possibility in the future, I requested a refund be made on compassionate grounds. |The answer was “no”!  Really??  Wow, we’re not about to spread recommendations for this place.

Thank you so much for the last 4 days. The park is so well maintained, the staff are so lovely and welcoming. Cannot rate the amenities highly enough, best showers out of any camp ground I have ever been too. We had a lovely view of the Causeway. The unpowered sites are very spacious. Thank you again, we will definately be back 👌
$57.00 a night during the week Packed in like sardines Would not recommend

A lovely park with lovely amenities. We had pretty ordinary weather during our stay but the kids still enjoyed themselves! A great pool and water park for the kids, my only suggestion would be for another enclosed camp kitchen down the back for the unpowered sites area to get out of the rain if it is bad weather.
We stayed in powered site. Amenities were great. Pool area excellent. Staff very friendly. Will visit again.

Very poor communication with the staff, the lady in the office is quite rude and should probably attend a lesson in how to communicate with clientele . Our car was broken down and unable to move, what a rig ma role that was to stay on the sight till the car was fixed, then they charged us an extra $10 on top of the Tarif for an emergency site. I hope that if you are ever travelling, and something goes wrong with your vehicle the place you're staying at acommadates and assists you.

Edit. July 2023.
We enjoyed our stay again here.
School holidays so its very busy.
Could do with more slab sites, especially when  its raining nothing worse than wet sandy/muddy feet.
Food vans are a nice touch when you don't want to cook.

March 2023.
We enjoyed our stay.
Staff friendly.
Pet friendly.
Close to the Causeway.
Plenty to do for kids.

Not to far from Yeppoon.

Site we were on was a dust bow (unpowered), turns out there was concrete 50mm under the dirt (hence no grass) so couldn't put any pegs in
This wasn't all unpowered sites.
Spoke to a groundsman who advised they don't escavate and they weren't to know. Caused considerable frustration for our camp through his attitude.
Lost an afternoon having to pack up and move to a different site.
Was also given incorrect information from the office staff causing other frustrating inconveniences.
The grounds and amenities are good with heaps to do for the kids, just avoid interactions with unhelpful staff
These guys are always great u meet them with a smile and Ur stay is always good!
Kat was very helpful at check in,staff and amenities very nice,look forward to our holiday here
